sound_type: loud guttural growl -- deep, rasping, sustained. 100-800 Hz. 1-5 seconds. Intimidation and territorial defense. E=6
ecosystem: Remote wilderness. High-elevation boreal and subalpine forest, alpine tundra. Requires vast undeveloped territory.
season: Year-round resident. Active in all seasons. Snow-dependent denning.

coordinates.description: Wolverine in North Cascades. Remote high-elevation wilderness.

taxonomy_note.description: Mustelidae returns from 185 (sea otter). Largest terrestrial mustelid. Fiercest predator relative to size.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Gulo gulo = 'glutton glutton'. From Latin gulo 'glutton'. Named for voracious appetite.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Gulo -- monotypic genus. One species worldwide.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Carnivora. Mustelidae returns. Largest terrestrial mustelid.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Mustelidae: second entry. Marine (otter) then terrestrial (wolverine).
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. 10-25 kg. Drives bears and wolves from kills.

song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Growl: 100-800 Hz. Shriek: 1,000-3,000 Hz. Bark: 500-1,500 Hz. Kit chatter: 1,000-4,000 Hz.
song_metrics.note_types: Intimidation growl (deep, sustained), shriek (extreme aggression), bark (alarm), kit chatter (close-range).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Sustained growl during standoff. May escalate to shriek.
song_metrics.timbre: Deep, guttural, rasping. Sounds larger than source. Acoustic deception.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=6. Disproportionate for body size (10-25 kg). Louder than expected.
song_metrics.context: Defending food cache, claiming kills, territorial disputes.
song_metrics.key_property: WOLVERINE. Guttural growl. E=6. Mustelidae returns. Fiercest relative to size. v1.49.357.

pelage.overall: Dark brown/black with distinctive pale lateral stripes. Dense, frost-resistant fur. Stocky build.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Dark brown body with pale cream/gold lateral stripes from shoulder to rump. Broad head. Short ears. Stocky.

physical_description.overall: 10-25 kg (22-55 lbs). Body length 65-107 cm. Short legs. Powerful build. Large paws.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Stocky, dark brown with pale lateral stripes. Short ears, broad head. Looks like a small bear.
physical_description.field_id: Stocky dark animal with pale side stripes, looks like a small bear or large marten. Extremely rare. If seen in WA, report to WDFW. Maintain distance.

nodes.title: Disproportionate Ferocity
nodes.description: 10-25 kg driving bears and wolves from kills. E=6 from a body that should produce E=3. Acoustic deception.

nodes.title: Wilderness Indicator Species
nodes.description: 100-500+ km2 territories. Where wolverines persist, wilderness is intact. 25-50 in WA. Climate vulnerable.

nodes.title: Mustelidae Returns -- Terrestrial Apex
nodes.description: Sea otter (marine, gentle) to wolverine (terrestrial, fierce). Same family, opposite temperaments.

cross_references.relationship: MUSTELIDAE PAIR! Marine (otter) vs. terrestrial (wolverine). Gentlest vs. fiercest.
BOTH SNOW SPECIALISTS! Shared North Cascades habitat. Both climate-vulnerable.
SIZE CONTRAST! Wolverine (10-25 kg) drives grizzly (180-360 kg) from kills.
POUND-FOR-POUND! 7-year band (E=7) and 15 kg animal (E=6). Maximum ferocity.
